name: Electric Beach (Kahe Point)
tagline: Warm water from the power plant outflow attracts spinner dolphins, sea turtles, and tropical fish in ridiculous abundance.
parking: Small gravel lot right off Farrington Hwy; comes and goes quickly
bestTime: Early morning for dolphins and calm water; weekdays to avoid crowds
talkStory: The Hawaiian name for this area is Kahe, meaning 'to flow.' The adjacent power plant's warm outflow raises water temps several degrees, attracting an unusual concentration of spinner dolphins, green sea turtles, and reef fish to one small area. Snorkel left toward the outflow pipe for the best action, but respect the current — it has pulled inexperienced swimmers out. Locals come at dawn before the wind chops up visibility; this is the only spot on Oahu where you can reliably swim near wild dolphins from shore.
